Software Review

Assignar Review (2026)

Construction workforce and equipment scheduling built for subcontractors and specialty contractors. Here's what it does well — and where it fits in your stack.

⭐ Rating: 4.2/5 — Best for subcontractors with complex workforce and equipment scheduling needs

Assignar is an Australian-origin construction operations platform that has built a strong following among subcontractors, specialty contractors, and civil construction companies. Its core strength is the intersection of workforce scheduling and equipment allocation — managing which workers with which qualifications and which equipment assets are assigned to which jobs on which days, with compliance and certification tracking woven throughout.

If your scheduling challenge is "I need a certified rigger and a 50-ton crane at the Riverside site Tuesday through Thursday, but Johnson's cert expires Wednesday and the crane has a maintenance check Friday" — Assignar is designed exactly for that problem.

Pricing

Plan Pricing What's included
Core Custom quote Workforce scheduling, basic equipment, timesheets
Pro Custom quote Adds compliance tracking, equipment maintenance, forms
Enterprise Custom quote Multi-company, advanced analytics, full API access

Pricing note: Assignar uses custom pricing based on company size and feature requirements. It's not priced as a budget tool — expect investment comparable to other mid-market construction operations platforms. The pricing is justified for companies with genuine workforce and equipment complexity; less so for simpler operations that could use a lighter tool.

What Assignar does well

Workforce scheduling with compliance awareness

Assignar's scheduling board shows workforce availability alongside worker qualifications, certifications, and expiration dates. When you schedule a worker for a job that requires a specific certification, the system automatically checks whether that certification is current — and flags it if not. This compliance-aware scheduling is a significant differentiator for trades with strict licensing requirements: crane operators, electricians, riggers, explosive handlers, and others.

The visual scheduling board is drag-and-drop, with workers as rows and days as columns. You can see at a glance who's available, who's allocated, who has certifications expiring soon, and where your gaps are. For companies managing 50–500+ field workers across multiple sites, this visibility is transformative compared to spreadsheets.

Equipment scheduling and maintenance

Equipment assets in Assignar are managed alongside workforce resources — same scheduling board, same visibility, same compliance awareness. Equipment can be flagged for maintenance windows, assigned service intervals, and tracked by location. When you schedule equipment, you can see its maintenance status and upcoming service requirements, preventing the scenario where a critical piece of equipment is committed to a job that conflicts with a scheduled service.

Digital forms and field operations

Assignar includes a form builder for creating digital checklists, inductions, safety forms, and daily reports. Workers complete these forms from the mobile app in the field, with GPS-stamped submissions that create a compliance record. Pre-start checklists for equipment, toolbox talk records, and site induction completions are all tracked in the system.

Timesheets and payroll data

Workers clock in and out through the mobile app, with time automatically allocated to the job they're scheduled on. Timesheets flow into payroll-ready export formats, with integration to several payroll platforms. For subcontractors managing complex award structures or union pay rates, the timesheet engine handles multiple pay rates by classification and job type.

Subcontractor management

Assignar is designed to work in environments where subcontractors and their workers need to be managed alongside direct employees. Subcontractor companies can be given limited access to schedule their workers, submit timesheets, and complete compliance forms — while the main contractor maintains oversight and compliance visibility across all parties on site.

Where Assignar falls short

Not a full project management platform

Assignar is an operations and workforce management tool, not a full construction project management platform. There's no document management, no RFI workflows, no cost tracking at the project level, no client portal. For companies that need those features, Assignar works best as a workforce/equipment layer alongside a PM platform like Procore or Autodesk Build.

Implementation complexity

Setting up Assignar properly — particularly the compliance framework, certification matrices, and equipment records — requires significant upfront work. Companies with large worker and equipment databases should budget 4–8 weeks for proper setup, ideally with implementation support from the Assignar team. Going live without proper configuration leads to adoption problems.

Mobile app reliability

Field worker experience with the mobile app is mixed — some operations report smooth adoption, others struggle with sync issues and UI complexity for lower-tech field workers. The app works best for supervisors and foremen doing scheduling tasks; for frontline workers doing basic clock-in and form completion, there can be a learning curve.

Assignar vs. alternatives

Tool Best For Key Difference
Assignar Subcontractors, civil, specialty trades Best workforce + equipment + compliance integration
Procore Workforce Procore users Integrated with Procore PM; less scheduling depth
Workyard GPS tracking + scheduling Simpler, cheaper, less compliance depth
busybusy GPS time tracking Stronger time tracking, less scheduling complexity
Buildforce Labor marketplace + scheduling Labor sourcing + management combined

Who should use Assignar?

Good fit: Specialty subcontractors managing 30+ field workers with licensing requirements (scaffolding, rigging, electrical, civil); companies with significant equipment fleets; civil construction companies managing complex multi-site deployments; any contractor where compliance tracking is a safety and legal obligation, not just a nice-to-have.

Not ideal for: General residential contractors who need full project management; companies with fewer than 20 workers (lighter tools are more cost-effective); businesses primarily doing service work rather than project-based construction.

The verdict

Assignar is a specialist tool for a specific problem — and for companies that have that problem, it solves it exceptionally well. Workforce scheduling with compliance awareness, integrated equipment management, and digital field operations make it the strongest platform in its category for subcontractors and specialty contractors managing complex deployment and compliance requirements.

If you're managing workers and equipment across multiple sites with certification and compliance obligations, Assignar deserves a serious evaluation. If you need a full project management platform, pair it with Procore or Autodesk Build — or evaluate whether a single platform that covers both layers fits your needs better.
